Diagnose your elsar setup

Source: R/elsar_check_setup.R
elsar_check_setup.Rd

Reports, tier by tier, what elsar needs and whether it is present on your machine, with the exact command to fix anything that is missing. It is safe to run any time: it makes no changes, needs no credentials, and never errors out - a failed probe is reported as a finding, not thrown.

Usage

elsar_check_setup(
  tiers = c("core", "protected_areas", "earth_engine"),
  quiet = FALSE
)

Arguments

tiers

Character vector naming which tiers to check. Any of "core", "protected_areas", "earth_engine". Defaults to all three.

quiet

Logical. If TRUE, suppress the printed report and only return the results invisibly. Default FALSE.

Value

Invisibly, a data.frame with one row per check and the columns tier, item, status ("ok", "missing", "warn", or "info"), detail, and fix. Returned invisibly so it can be inspected programmatically while the human-readable report prints to the console.

Details

elsar has three tiers with different setup requirements (see the package's SUPPORT.md):

  • Core - boundaries, planning units, normalisation, zone builders from local rasters, and plotting. Needs only the geospatial R stack (GDAL, GEOS, PROJ, installed with sf/terra). Works offline.

  • Protected areas - make_protected_areas(from_wdpca = TRUE), which needs wdpar, a Chromium browser, and the libarchive system library.

  • Earth Engine - the download_* functions, which need reticulate, googledrive, a conda/Python environment with earthengine-api, and a Google Earth Engine account with an approved Cloud project.

If you are filing a bug report about the download_* functions, paste the output of elsar_check_setup() into the report.

Examples

# Safe to run anywhere - no changes, no credentials required.
elsar_check_setup()
#> 
#> elsar setup check
#> =================
#> 
#> Core - ready
#>   [ ok ] R                        R 4.6.1
#>   [ ok ] sf                       sf 1.1.1 (GDAL 3.8.4, GEOS 3.12.1, PROJ 9.4.0)
#>   [ ok ] terra                    terra 1.9.34 (GDAL 3.8.4)
#>   [ ok ] elsar                    elsar 0.4.0
#> 
#> Protected areas - ready
#>   [ ok ] wdpar                    wdpar 1.3.9
#>   [ ok ] Chromium browser         /usr/bin/google-chrome
#>   [ ok ] libarchive               archive package present (libarchive available)
#> 
#> Earth Engine - 1 item needs attention
#>   [ ok ] reticulate               reticulate 1.46.0
#>   [ ok ] conda                    /usr/share/miniconda/bin/conda
#>   [MISS] Earth Engine conda env   no conda env named one of: elsar_ee, ee_compat, ee, gee, earthengine, earth-engine
#>          -> run elsar_setup_gee() to create it and install earthengine-api
#>   [ ok ] googledrive              googledrive 2.1.2
#>   [ .. ] Google Drive auth        no Drive token cached yet
#>          -> you'll be prompted to authenticate in the browser on first use
#>   [ .. ] Earth Engine auth        no Earth Engine credentials found yet
#>          -> run elsar_setup_gee(), or authenticate on first use of a download_* function
#>   [ ok ] gdalsrsinfo              /usr/bin/gdalsrsinfo
#> 
#> 1 missing, 0 warning(s). See the '->' lines above for fixes.
#> 

# Just the core geospatial stack:
elsar_check_setup(tiers = "core")
#> 
#> elsar setup check
#> =================
#> 
#> Core - ready
#>   [ ok ] R                        R 4.6.1
#>   [ ok ] sf                       sf 1.1.1 (GDAL 3.8.4, GEOS 3.12.1, PROJ 9.4.0)
#>   [ ok ] terra                    terra 1.9.34 (GDAL 3.8.4)
#>   [ ok ] elsar                    elsar 0.4.0
#> 
#> All checked items are present.
#> 

# Capture the results without printing:
res <- elsar_check_setup(quiet = TRUE)
res[res$status != "ok", ]
#>            tier                   item  status
#> 10 Earth Engine Earth Engine conda env missing
#> 12 Earth Engine      Google Drive auth    info
#> 13 Earth Engine      Earth Engine auth    info
#>                                                                                detail
#> 10 no conda env named one of: elsar_ee, ee_compat, ee, gee, earthengine, earth-engine
#> 12                                                          no Drive token cached yet
#> 13                                              no Earth Engine credentials found yet
#>                                                                             fix
#> 10               run elsar_setup_gee() to create it and install earthengine-api
#> 12               you'll be prompted to authenticate in the browser on first use
#> 13 run elsar_setup_gee(), or authenticate on first use of a download_* function
